assertions.title: Treasury coin references resolve through core coin endpoints

steps.observed: Returns coin with id='bitcoin', symbol='btc', name='BTC'

steps.expected: Government treasury entity returns coin_id values
Treasury coin_id resolves with same identity as market endpoints
steps.observed: Both entities return 'bitcoin' which resolves through /coins/bitcoin with matching id, symbol, and name

assertions.title: Equivalent addresses do not split identity by casing

steps.expected: Returns same pool as lowercase OR rejects consistently
Returns same pool as lowercase OR rejects consistently
Returns same pools as lowercase OR rejects consistently
Returns same coin as lowercase OR rejects consistently
steps.observed: Returns coin id='usd-coin' (normalizes to lowercase internally)

issues: Inconsistent address normalization across onchain endpoints: lowercase pool addresses work, uppercase/mixed-case return 404 or empty results

Inconsistent behavior between contract route (/coins/ethereum/contract/...) and onchain pool route (/onchain/networks/eth/pools/...): contract route normalizes addresses (accepts uppercase), onchain route does not normalize
Pool detail endpoint returns 'not found' error for uppercase/mixed-case, while pool multi endpoint returns empty array for same case variants - inconsistent error handling

frictions: Onchain pool endpoints do not normalize addresses before lookup, causing equivalent addresses to fail inconsistently
Contract route normalizes to lowercase before lookup, creating inconsistent behavior compared to onchain routes
blockers: The onchain pool endpoint address handling needs to normalize addresses (trim + lowercase) before identity matching, similar to how contract endpoints handle it
summary: Tested 2 cross-area assertions. VAL-CROSS-013 (Treasury coin references resolve through core coin endpoints): PASS. Treasury entities (strategy, el-salvador) both return coin_id='bitcoin' which successfully resolves through /coins/bitcoin with matching identity (id, symbol, name). VAL-CROSS-023 (Equivalent addresses do not split identity by casing): FAIL. Onchain pool endpoints accept lowercase addresses but reject uppercase/mixed-case variants with 404 errors or empty results. Contract endpoints (/coins/ethereum/contract/...) correctly normalize addresses and accept all case variants. This is inconsistent behavior that violates the assertion - equivalent addresses should either resolve to the same canonical entity or be rejected consistently if normalization is not supported.
